title: salix raupii
intro: Salix raupii, also known as Raup's willow, is a species of willow that is native to New Zealand. The plant has been found to contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. The medicinal properties of Salix raupii have been shown to help reduce inflammation and relieve pain, making it a potential natural remedy for conditions such as arthritis. Salix raupii can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used to promote overall health and well-being.
